notes: Ukrainian independent watchmaker known for artistic, avant-garde designs and custom builds. Produces limited-edition timepieces with distinctive aesthetic.
Cuban watchmaker founded in 1882, known for classic dress watches and chronographs. Historical brand with modern independent production.
Israeli independent watchmaker known for custom and bespoke mechanical watches with artistic design elements.
Historic Russian watch manufacturer known for mechanical watches and pocket watches with Soviet heritage.

Founded in the 1950s, Ollech & Wajs is among the oldest continuously operating watch brands in Israel, established during the formative years of the state and known for robust professional and tool watches with a following in military and diving communities. The brand has an established Tel Aviv presence. For groups seeking non-European itineraries, Israel offers a genuinely different cultural context. Group visitability and current operational arrangements require direct confirmation.
Historic Russian state watchmaker known for pilot and aviation watches with mechanical movements, currently producing homage and vintage-inspired models.

One of the world's oldest watch factories, tracing origins to a 1721 imperial decree by Peter the Great. The Raketa brand launched in 1961 and still produces its own movements entirely in-house in Peterhof, near St. Petersburg. Factory visits possible.
Russian independent brand specializing in vintage-inspired mechanical watches using Soviet-era design heritage.
Often described as the first watch brand born and built in Brazil. Collaborates with Swiss-Brazilian expert Angelique Chappuis in Neuchatel for development.
Russian state-owned manufacturer with deep military heritage. Produces durable mechanical watches including iconic Amphibia and Komandirskie dive models.
Ukrainian independent brand established in 2003. Known for affordable mechanical watches drawing on Soviet military traditions, using Vostok and ETA movements.
Russian watch factory and brand with history dating to Soviet era. Known for mechanical watches and tool watches produced in Zlatoust.
Russian watch brand associated with Zlatoust factory heritage. Produces mechanical watches with historical Soviet manufacturing roots.
